text.title: LB Session Persistence Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure to apply the lb-session-persistence methodology end-to-end.

step.name: check-externalize
step.action: If session lives in Redis / DB already, set `method=externalized-no-sticky` and STOP — remove any existing sticky config.
step.decision-gate: If externalizable in ≤ 1 sprint, schedule the work + use temporary stickiness only.

step.name: check-client-environment
step.input: Client mix (direct / NAT / VPN) + autoscaling policy.
step.action: If autoscaling = true OR NAT-behind-LB = true → ip-hash forbidden. Pick cookie-based.

step.name: pick-cookie-vs-iphash
step.action: Cookie-based default (LB-inserted for OSS HAProxy / Nginx Plus; app-managed for OSS Nginx). IP-hash only on a fixed pool with direct clients.

step.name: bound-stickiness
step.action: Set `maxidle 3600 maxlife 86400` (or zone `expire 1h`). Configure `invalidate_on_removal = true` so backend draining clears stickies.

step.name: emit-and-validate
step.action: Render `templates/sticky-haproxy.cfg` or `templates/sticky-nginx.conf`. Run `validate-lb-session-persistence.py --file artefact.json`. Smoke test: 10 requests from one client land on the same backend; after restart of that backend, the next request lands on a peer.
